evidence_summary: GEO/SOFT title is 'Performance differences in spatial transcriptomics platforms identify Treg biology in human inflammatory bowel disease [CosMx]'. Public SOFT lists 60 sample records on GPL33484 NanoString CosMx SMI, BioProject PRJNA1373285, and series-level supplementary file GSE312415_files.tar.gz. Sample titles include 16 CosMx1k I*/ITDF00A or RSDF00A tissue IDs, 4 CosMx6k I*/RSDF00A IDs, 8 Colon_HC 302c samples, preparation-condition samples, and 2 CosMx2.0 rectum inflamed samples.
overlap_assessment: CosMx record is a platform component of the same IBD spatial benchmark as DS119; not a separate cohort family.
manual_review_notes: Use DS118 as the countable family representative if a binary countability decision is required. This does not make DS118 a parent dataset; it is only the representative row for de-duplicated cohort counting.

evidence_summary: GEO/SOFT title is 'Performance differences in spatial transcriptomics platforms identify Treg biology in human inflammatory bowel disease [Xenium]'. Public SOFT lists 32 sample records on GPL33762 Xenium In Situ Analyzer, BioProject PRJNA1373279, and series-level supplementary file GSE312420_RAW.tar. Sample titles include 16 Xenium337 multi-tissue samples plus repeated runs for four shared rectal IDs using NS/WS Xenium337, Xenium colon, and Xenium5K panels.
overlap_assessment: Xenium record is a sibling platform component of the same IBD spatial benchmark as DS118 and shares public tissue/section-style IDs with DS118.
manual_review_notes: Do not count DS119 as an additional independent cohort when DS118 is counted as the family representative. Keep as a primary data row for discoverability and platform-specific access.

evidence_summary: Direct GEO sample-title parsing found 20 shared tissue/section-style base IDs after removing platform suffixes: I0262 ITDF00A; I0262 RSDF00A; I0275 ITDF00A; I0275 RSDF00A; I0276 RSDF00A; I0277 RSDF00A; I0278 ITDF00A; I0284 RSDF00A; I0286 RSDF00A; I0294 ITDF00A; I0294 RSDF00A; I0303 RSDF00A; I0310 ITDF00A; I0310 RSDF00A; I0321 ITDF00A; I0321 RSDF00A; I0355 RSDF00A; I0380 RSDF00A; I0387 RSDF00A; I0400 RSCF00A. The first 16 IDs correspond to CosMx1k and Xenium337 multi-tissue comparisons; four IDs correspond to CosMx6k and multiple Xenium panel/segmentation records.
overlap_assessment: Confirmed overlap for a core subset at public tissue/section-style ID level; not excluded. Full-row overlap remains partial because each accession also contains platform-specific or processing-specific records not mirrored one-to-one.
manual_review_notes: The public IDs strongly support shared subjects/tissues for the core platform benchmark. They do not prove whether the assayed material was the same physical section, adjacent serial sections, or separate sections from the same block.

evidence_summary: Two shared rectal Crohn IDs have discordant disease-state labels between CosMx GEO sample titles and Xenium/supplement metadata: I0294 RSDF00A is Rectum_CD_Inflamed in GSE312415 title but Rectum_CD_Noninflamed in GSE312420 and supplementary tables; I0303 RSDF00A is Rectum_CD_Noninflamed in GSE312415 title but Rectum_CD_Inflamed in GSE312420 and supplementary tables.
overlap_assessment: Overlap remains supported by shared IDs, but disease-state labels for these two records need manual review before downstream phenotype use.
manual_review_notes: Do not use sample title disease-state labels blindly for these two IDs. Prefer publication supplement/sample sheet after manual confirmation.

evidence_summary: Nature Communications 2026 abstract states the study used over 100 intestinal tissue sections from non-diseased controls and UC/CD patients and systematically evaluated two imaging-based spatial transcriptomics platforms. Supplementary Dataset 4/5 tables explicitly list paired CosMx and Xenium metrics for the same sample IDs: 16 'CosMx MT, Xenium MT' samples and 4 'CosMx 6K, Xenium 5K' samples.
overlap_assessment: Publication context confirms this is a single platform-benchmark study family with paired/sibling platform comparisons, not two independent recruitment cohorts.
manual_review_notes: Article page is an early-access Nature page and GEO citation fields may still be incomplete. The supplement is stronger evidence for sample-ID pairing than article prose alone.

evidence_summary: CosMx and Xenium are parallel sibling platform records under one benchmark family. There is no public parent accession row in the registry, and neither DS118 nor DS119 is a strict parent of the other.
overlap_assessment: component_of_dataset_id should remain blank for both rows. Use shared study_family plus countability flags to prevent double counting.
manual_review_notes: If a future parent benchmark row is created, component_of_dataset_id could be revisited. With the current two-row sibling model, leaving component links blank is cleaner.
